selection: Fermi estimates for every pro/contra argument of the RSI / overstimulation / zukunft.com discussion, with reproducible calc-validation chains
description: Encodes the pro and contra arguments of the thread into the zukunft.com graph. Each value carries a provenance qualifier triple - 'measured value' for figures taken from a source, 'assumed value' for set Fermi inputs - so that a set assumption can never be mistaken for a measurement. v0.0.4 additions: the dominant 'overstimulation gap' is now decomposed into 'intensity gap' x 'vulnerability gap'; the upper-bound (gap 10) sign-flip scenario is encoded as reproducible values and calc chains rather than prose; the previously dead 'confidence' inputs now feed a confidence-discounted RSI expected harm; the empirical/normative split is attached to the normative value entries; and an enforcement false-positive (over-blocking) cost is added to the contra side of the rollback balance. Every calc-validation entry is reproduced by check.py and is order-independent of the input values.

words.name: measured
words.description: provenance qualifier: the number is taken from a cited source and is in principle verifiable

words.name: assumed
words.description: provenance qualifier: the number is a set Fermi input, not a measurement; the conclusion may hinge on it

words.name: status
words.description: as in status quo: the present-day un-intervened state

words.name: rollback
words.description: reduction of digital overstimulation, e.g. via content regulation / press-code enforcement

words.name: measurement
words.description: the act of running a study to estimate the sign-determining factor

words.name: neonatal
words.description: during the critical developmental window; the mouse study used p10 pups

words.name: readiness
words.description: willingness to subject oneself to the effort of checking, as opposed to the ability to do so

words.name: DALY
words.description: disability-adjusted life year (UN); one lost year of healthy life

words.name: HTP
words.description: Happy Time Point; DALY extended to Maslow levels 1-2, impact-weighted reach

words.name: dB
words.description: decibel, sound pressure level

words.name: RSI
words.description: recursive self-improvement: AI that builds the next, better AI generation - distinct from intra-task self-verification

words.name: verification
words.description: intra-task self-checking: extra test/correction code within a single task

words.name: direct
words.description: base modifier for directional properties

words.name: reduction
words.description: base concept for a decrease in a specific friction quantity

words.name: runaway
words.description: uncontrolled compounding feedback loop

words.name: mitigation
words.description: actions taken to reduce the severity or probability of a negative event

words.name: efficacy
words.description: the performance metrics of a specific intervention framework

words.name: audio
words.description: sound-related metric components

words.name: normative
words.description: value judgement rather than empirical observation

words.name: empirical
words.description: derived from observation or experiment

words.name: overblocking
words.description: enforcement false positive: legitimate content wrongly hidden by press-code / one-click-hide enforcement

triples.name: overstimulation gap
triples.description: orders-of-magnitude gap in intensity x vulnerability between the neonatal-mouse model dose and adult status-quo media use

triples.name: conversion ratio
triples.description: WHO budget conversion factor to HTP

triples.name: flip probability
triples.description: probability that the measurement actually flips the decision sign

triples.name: avoided wrong decision
triples.description: harm of the wrong decision that the measurement avoids

triples.name: coercion structure
triples.description: lever: mandatory justification before voting; forces checking instead of inviting it

triples.name: identity group
triples.description: lever: peer norm makes checking a status marker

triples.name: direct incentive
triples.description: lever: ranking / reputation / money reward for rare-but-right

triples.name: friction reduction
triples.description: lever: remove distraction; acts on opportunity not motivation - the petition's mechanism

triples.name: lever score
triples.description: effect x reach product, ordinal, for ranking the levers only

triples.name: intensity gap
triples.description: dose ratio: neonatal-mouse model intensity (70 dB + flashing light, 6h/day) vs adult status-quo media exposure

triples.name: vulnerability gap
triples.description: developmental-window ratio: neonatal (p10) critical-period plasticity vs adult susceptibility

triples.name: upper bound
triples.description: optimistic-for-rollback gap scenario: overstimulation gap = 10 instead of 100

triples.name: confidence discounted RSI harm
triples.description: RSI expected harm multiplied by the confidence in its source, so a low-confidence risk figure does not dominate the ledger at face value

triples.name: overblocking cost
triples.description: expected HTP cost of legitimate speech/information wrongly suppressed by the rollback's enforcement mechanism (e.g. Ein-Klick-Verbergen over-application)

sources.name: Christakis 2012 mouse overstimulation
sources.description: Christakis DA, Ramirez JSB, Ramirez JM (2012). Overstimulation of newborn mice leads to behavioral differences and deficits in cognitive performance. Scientific Reports 2:546. p10 mice, 70 dB audio + flashing lights, 6 h/day, 42 days; overstimulated mice performed significantly worse on Light-Dark Latency, Elevated Plus Maze, Novel Object Recognition and Barnes Maze.

sources.name: Anthropic RSI warning (heise 2026)
sources.description: heise online: Skynet-Szenario - Anthropic warnt vor KI, die sich selbst entwickelt. Source of the RSI definition used to separate recursive self-improvement from intra-task self-verification.

sources.name: WHO cost-effectiveness threshold
sources.description: Health-economic convention: an intervention's cost per DALY averted, here a set ~50000 CHF/DALY threshold used only to convert study cost into HTP.

sources.name: Decision analysis convention
sources.description: General decision-analysis practice: identify sign-determining assumptions and prioritise measuring them.

formulas.name: status quo harm weight formula
formulas.expression: "status quo harm weight" = "model harm weight" / "overstimulation gap"

formulas.name: status quo harm per person formula
formulas.expression: "status quo harm per person" = "status quo harm weight" * "exposure duration"

formulas.name: status quo total harm formula
formulas.expression: "status quo total harm" = "status quo harm per person" * "population"

formulas.name: rollback net balance formula
formulas.expression: "rollback net balance" = "status quo total harm" - "rollback cost"

formulas.name: value of information ratio formula
formulas.expression: "value of information ratio" = "value of information" / "study cost"

formulas.name: verification net balance formula
formulas.expression: "verification net balance" = ("RSI expected harm" * "verification mitigation efficacy") - "verification cost"

values.note: Normative value: the disutility of lost autonomy is a value judgement, not a measured quantity.
Normative value: the HTP cost assigned to misuse reflects a value judgement about its severity.
Dose ratio component of the overstimulation gap.
Developmental-window component. Product with intensity gap = 100, the lower-bound overstimulation gap.
Upper-bound scenario: status-quo harm is 10x larger than the lower-bound case.
Assumed share of hide-actions that wrongly target legitimate content.
Normative value: HTP cost of the suppressed legitimate information if over-blocking were certain.

calc-validation.note: CONTRA the status-quo-is-harmful thesis: lower bound, gap factor 100. With gap 10 the result would be 0.02.

calc-validation.formula: status quo harm per person formula

calc-validation.note: PRO the thesis: even at the conservative lower bound the diffuse mass harm is ~6.4M HTP, because reach is huge while per-capita weight is tiny. This is the strength of the HTP logic - it makes sub-threshold mass harm visible.
CONTRA naive rollback: primum non nocere requires the intervention's own cost (now ~5.2M HTP, including ~1M HTP of enforcement over-blocking) be counted against the harm it averts.

KEY RESULT - sign uncertain: at lower bound (gap 100) net = 6.4M - 5.2M = 1.2M, even closer to a wash once the enforcement over-blocking cost is included. At upper bound (gap 10) status-quo harm = 64M, net = 64M - 5.2M = 58.8M, a clear rollback win. The decision still hinges on the single un-measured overstimulation gap.
Harm of measuring: ~70 HTP of foregone alternative use of the research budget. Observation study, so participant harm ~0.

PRO measuring first: benefit ~2.5M HTP vs cost ~70 HTP gives ~35700:1 (the discussion's spoken ~25000:1 was a looser estimate; recomputed here). Unlike rollback and petition, the SIGN of measuring is robust. Caveat: holds only if the study design can actually flip the sign - a confounded observational study flips nothing and the ratio collapses.

Documented dose of the mouse model: 6 h/day x 42 days = 252 h at 70 dB + flashing light, neonatal. The argument the source does NOT support: extrapolating this non-normative neonatal extreme to adult status-quo media use. Datapoint, not keystone.
Readiness lever ranking (effect x reach, ordinal). Coercion/structure highest - forces checking, reaches the unwilling.

CONTRA the petition: friction reduction (the petition's mechanism) ranks last - it acts on opportunity, not motivation, reaching only the already-willing. Three stronger levers are already inside zukunft.com.
Fermi risk baseline for recursive self-improvement runaway loops prior to engineering safeguards.

PRO Intra-task verification frameworks: Although implementation costs 200k HTP in latency/overhead, filtering 90% of recursive failures yields a highly positive net balance of ~2.05M HTP, justifying structural enforcement over reliance on pure regulation.
The current model indicates that the overstimulation gap is the dominant sign-determining assumption and therefore the highest-value measurement target.

Decomposition of the dominant assumption: gap 100 = intensity 10 x vulnerability 10. Makes auditable which half (dose vs developmental window) drives the sign. Sensitivity analysis should target whichever factor is more measurable - intensity is observable now, vulnerability needs a developmental study.

calc-validation.formula: status quo harm per person formula

calc-validation.note: PRO rollback at the upper bound: 64M HTP of diffuse mass harm.

The RSI source carries low confidence (0.3), so its 2.5M HTP expected harm enters the ledger discounted to ~750k HTP. This keeps a single low-confidence frontier-risk figure from dominating empirically better-supported terms.
CONTRA rollback (new term): the enforcement mechanism itself suppresses some legitimate content. Expected cost ~1M HTP, previously omitted - its omission biased rollback net upward.
Upper-bound counterpart to the KEY RESULT, now reproducible rather than narrated in prose: gap 10 -> net 58.8M HTP, a clear rollback win. The lower- and upper-bound nets bracket the decision.
